# mxl-schemas

> Canonical JSON Schema definitions for the mxl* tool family ([mxlpy](https://github.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/mxlpy), [mxlweb](https://github.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/mxlweb)). Provides three model formats — kinetic (reactions + stoichiometry), ODE (direct dx/dt encoding), and steady-state (algebraic outputs) — all expressed as trees of math nodes. Maintained by the Computational Biology group at RWTH Aachen.

## Envelope and discriminator

Every `.mxl.json` file shares one outer shape. A required top-level `kind` field discriminates the formulation, so consumers select the right schema without sniffing structure:

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/mxl-schemas/main/v1/<kind>-model.schema.json",
  "spec_version": "1.0",
  "kind": "kinetic | ode | steady-state",
  "model_id": "my_model",
  "description": "optional",
  "model": { ... }
}
```

## Model formats

### Kinetic model (`kind: "kinetic"`, `kinetic-model.schema.json`)

Encodes dx/dt as **reactions × stoichiometry** (dx/dt = N*v). Each reaction has a rate expression (fn) and a per-variable stoichiometry map. The solver computes time derivatives from the reaction network.

Sections: `variables`, `parameters`, `reactions`, `derived`, `readouts`.

### ODE model (`kind: "ode"`, `ode-model.schema.json`)

Encodes dx/dt **directly on each variable** via a `fn` field. No reactions or stoichiometry — the derivative is a single expression tree. Useful for models not naturally expressed as reaction networks.

Sections: `variables` (with fn), `parameters`, `derived`, `readouts`.

### Steady-state model (`kind: "steady-state"`, `steady-state-model.schema.json`)

Algebraic model: no state variables and no time integration. The `derived` quantities are closed-form outputs of the parameters; any parameter can be swept as the independent axis. `readouts` is omitted because there are no dynamics for a report-only quantity to stay out of.

Sections: `parameters`, `derived`.

## Presentation metadata

Every entity accepts optional presentation fields (a bare math-only file still validates):

- `displayName` — human-readable label.
- `texName` — LaTeX rendering of the symbol.
- `slider` — `{ min, max, step, desc? }` interactive-slider config on `variable` / `parameter`; bounds are strings to preserve authored precision.

## Math node tree

All expressions (rates, derived, readouts, initial values, stoichiometry, derivatives) are stored as recursive trees of nodes. Each node has a `type` discriminator:

| type     | value       | Children        | Meaning                           |
| -------- | ----------- | --------------- | --------------------------------- |
| `Num`    | `number`    | —               | Numeric literal                   |
| `Name`   | `string`    | —               | Reference to a variable/parameter |
| `Bool`   | `boolean`   | —               | Boolean literal                   |
| unary    | —           | `child`         | Single operand (e.g. negation)    |
| binary   | —           | `left`, `right` | Two operands (`Pow`, `Implies`)   |
| nary     | —           | `children`      | N operands (e.g. `+`, `*`)        |

`Log` and `Sqrt` carry a `child` plus a `base` field (base/degree).

## Example: kinetic model

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/mxl-schemas/main/v1/kinetic-model.schema.json",
  "spec_version": "1.0",
  "kind": "kinetic",
  "model_id": "simple_enzyme",
  "description": "Michaelis-Menten enzyme kinetics",
  "model": {
    "variables": {
      "S": { "value": { "type": "Num", "value": 10.0 } },
      "P": { "value": { "type": "Num", "value": 0.0 } }
    },
    "parameters": {
      "vmax": { "value": { "type": "Num", "value": 5.0 } },
      "km":   { "value": { "type": "Num", "value": 2.0 } }
    },
    "reactions": {
      "v1": {
        "fn": {
          "type": "Divide",
          "children": [
            {
              "type": "Mul",
              "children": [
                { "type": "Name", "value": "vmax" },
                { "type": "Name", "value": "S" }
              ]
            },
            {
              "type": "Add",
              "children": [
                { "type": "Name", "value": "km" },
                { "type": "Name", "value": "S" }
              ]
            }
          ]
        },
        "stoichiometry": {
          "S": { "type": "Num", "value": -1 },
          "P": { "type": "Num", "value": 1 }
        }
      }
    },
    "derived": {
      "flux": {
        "fn": { "type": "Name", "value": "v1" }
      }
    },
    "readouts": {
      "total": {
        "fn": {
          "type": "Add",
          "children": [
            { "type": "Name", "value": "S" },
            { "type": "Name", "value": "P" }
          ]
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Example: ODE model

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/mxl-schemas/main/v1/ode-model.schema.json",
  "spec_version": "1.0",
  "kind": "ode",
  "model_id": "decay",
  "description": "Simple exponential decay",
  "model": {
    "variables": {
      "x": {
        "value": { "type": "Num", "value": 1.0 },
        "fn": {
          "type": "Mul",
          "children": [
            { "type": "Num", "value": -0.5 },
            { "type": "Name", "value": "x" }
          ]
        }
      }
    },
    "parameters": {},
    "derived": {},
    "readouts": {}
  }
}
```

## Example: steady-state model

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/mxl-schemas/main/v1/steady-state-model.schema.json",
  "spec_version": "1.0",
  "kind": "steady-state",
  "model_id": "mm_rate",
  "description": "Michaelis-Menten rate as a function of its parameters",
  "model": {
    "parameters": {
      "vmax": { "value": { "type": "Num", "value": 5.0 } },
      "km":   { "value": { "type": "Num", "value": 2.0 } },
      "S":    {
        "value": { "type": "Num", "value": 1.0 },
        "slider": { "min": "0", "max": "10", "step": "0.1" }
      }
    },
    "derived": {
      "v": {
        "fn": {
          "type": "Divide",
          "children": [
            {
              "type": "Mul",
              "children": [
                { "type": "Name", "value": "vmax" },
                { "type": "Name", "value": "S" }
              ]
            },
            {
              "type": "Add",
              "children": [
                { "type": "Name", "value": "km" },
                { "type": "Name", "value": "S" }
              ]
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Installation & usage

### npm (schema package)

```bash
npm install @computational-biology-aachen/mxl-schemas
```

### Python (mxlpy import/export)

```python
import mxlpy

mxlpy.save(model, "model.mxl.json")
model = mxlpy.load("model.mxl.json")
```

### Validation

```bash
# Python
pip install check-jsonschema
check-jsonschema --schemafile v1/kinetic-model.schema.json path/to/model.mxl.json

# Node
npm install -g ajv-cli
ajv validate -s v1/kinetic-model.schema.json -d path/to/model.mxl.json
```

### Editor integration (VS Code)

Add to `.vscode/settings.json`:

```json
{
  "json.schemas": [
    {
      "fileMatch": ["*.mxl.json"],
      "url": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/mxl-schemas/main/v1/kinetic-model.schema.json"
    }
  ]
}
```

## MCP integration

Use [mxl-mcp](https://computational-biology-aachen.github.io/mxl-mcp/mxlpy/latest) for LLM-driven model building and simulation via the Model Context Protocol.

## Tool family

- [mxlpy](https://github.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/mxlpy) — Python mechanistic learning library (ODEs + ML)
- [mxlbricks](https://github.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/mxl-bricks) — Composable reaction building blocks
- [mxlmodels](https://github.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/mxl-models) — Pre-defined model library (generated from mxlbricks)
- [mxlweb](https://github.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/mxl-web) — Browser-based ODE solver (SvelteKit + WASM)
- [pysbml](https://github.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/pysbml) — SBML import/export bridge
- [parameteriser](https://github.com/Computational-Biology-Aachen/parameteriser) — Kinetic parameter lookup (BRENDA DB)
